context:
Page 33, Section 9 (Concluding remarks), second open question paragraph

"Similarly, strong duality is only established for convex h and affine cost, and we wonder whether it also holds in more general situations."
title: Strong duality beyond affine costs in distorted optimal transport
latex:
Determine whether strong duality holds for distorted optimal transport beyond the affine-cost case.

Let \(\mu,\nu\) be Borel probability measures on \(\mathbb{R}\). Let \(h:[0,1]\to[0,1]\) be a distortion function (increasing with \(h(0)=0\), \(h(1)=1\)), and let \(\mathcal{X}\) be the set of bounded real-valued random variables on an atomless probability space. Define the distorted expectation \(\mathbb{E}_h\) by
\[
\mathbb{E}_h[Z]=\int_0^{\infty} h(\mathbb{P}(Z>x))\,dx+\int_{-\infty}^0 \big(h(\mathbb{P}(Z>x))-1\big)\,dx,
\]
for bounded \(Z\in\mathcal{X}\). For a measurable cost \(c:\mathbb{R}^2\to\mathbb{R}\), consider the primal distorted optimal transport problem
\[
\mathrm{P}(c,h;\mu,\nu)=\inf\{\mathbb{E}_h[c(X,Y)]:\ X,Y\in\mathcal{X},\ X\sim\mu,\ Y\sim\nu\}.
\]
Define the Kantorovich-type constraint set
\[
S_c=\{(\varphi,\psi):\ \varphi,\psi:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\ \text{measurable and }\ \varphi(x)+\psi(y)\le c(x,y)\ \forall x,y\in\mathbb{R}\}.
\]
Let \(X_\mu\sim\mu\) and \(X_\nu\sim\nu\) be arbitrary random variables with these laws. The corresponding (nonlinear) dual value is
\[
\mathrm{D}(c,h;\mu,\nu)=\sup_{(\varphi,\psi)\in S_c}\ \big\{\mathbb{E}_h[\varphi(X_\mu)]+\mathbb{E}_h[\psi(X_\nu)]\big\}.
\]
It is known in the paper that \(\mathrm{P}(c,h;\mu,\nu)=\mathrm{D}(c,h;\mu,\nu)\) holds when \(c\) is affine, i.e., \(c(x,y)=\alpha+\beta x+\gamma y\), but strong duality is not established for more general costs.

Problem: Characterize classes of costs \(c\) and distortion functions \(h\) (e.g., convex \(h\)) for which strong duality holds, i.e.,
\[
\mathrm{P}(c,h;\mu,\nu)=\mathrm{D}(c,h;\mu,\nu)
\]
for all Borel probability measures \(\mu,\nu\) on \(\mathbb{R}\), or provide counterexamples showing failure of strong duality beyond the affine-cost case.

open_problem_quote: "Similarly, strong duality is only established for convex h and affine cost, and we wonder whether it also holds in more general situations."
